content.title: Guidelines For Ranked Battle
content.body: Rules and Guidelines Select Between Single or Double Battles First Player to Defeat All  Pokemon Wins Player Loses When Time Runs Out Time Limit for Move Selection is Also Set Battle Becomes Once the Time Limit Runs Out Pokemon or Items Cannot Be Used Twice Eligible Pokemon List Determines Pokemon

content.title: Select Between Single or Double Battles
content.body: Battle Guidelines Single Battle ・ Select 3 Pokemon From Your Team to Battle ・Engage in 1v1 Battles Against Opposing Player Double Battle ・ Select 4 Pokemon From Your Team to Battle ・Engage in 2v2 Battles Against Opposing Player

content.title: First Player to Defeat All Opposing Pokemon Wins
content.body: The first player to beat all the Pokemon from the opposing team wins the battle.

content.title: Player Loses When Time Runs Out During the Round
content.body: There is a total of 7 minutes allotted for each player during battles, which becomes the set period of whether they will choose a move or switch to another Pokemon.

content.title: Time Limit for Move Selection is Also Set
content.body: There is also a total of 45 seconds allotted for players when they are choosing a move for their Pokemon to use in battle.

content.title: Battle Becomes a Draw Once the Time Limit for the Match Runs Out
content.body: When both players still have remaining Pokemon and the time limit of 20 minutes run out, the battle is ultimately decided as a draw . It will also be concluded as a draw, regardless of the number of Pokemon remaining on the opposing teams.

content.title: Pokemon or Items Cannot Be Used Twice
content.body: You cannot use the same Pokemon or Items twice in your team. This means that each of your Pokemon in your team should be different from one another , and all items that your Pokemon are holding should be different as well.

content.title: Pokemon That Can Compete Will Depend on Eligible Pokemon List
content.body: There are also guidelines on which Pokemon you can use during Ranked Battles. The list can be seen once you've pressed the + Button on your switch when the option to Begin Matchmaking appears.

content.title: Ranked Battles
content.body: Ranked Battles is the main competitive mode in the game. In this mode, you will be matched against player with the similar skill level as yours. You will start out in the Poke Ball Tier and climb up the rankings as you win battles.

content.title: Casual Battles
content.body: Casual Battles is the game's unranked mode . Here, your wins and losses are not recorded, so you may use this feature to freely test out your team composition or if you just want to enjoy battles without worrying about your rank.

content.title: Private Battles
content.body: Private Battles allow you to set up rooms where you can play with your friends. Room IDs are generated here to join private sessions or you may also be invited to another player's room.

content.title: Online Competitions
content.body: Online Competitions are battles where there rules and battle formats are set and are held during seasons. In this mode, you need to sign up to participate in an ongoing Online Competition before you can start battling with other players.
